original_abstract: Research-policy dialogues may take place not only at the national and EU level, but also at the local level, especially in cities. However, research on how local policymakers call on researchers, or how researchers try to influence the policymaking process in cities is scarce. This chapter explores research-policy dialogues in European cities: their forms, contents and use. Furthermore, it addresses the question of how local research-policy dialogues relate to national policies and what role the EU plays in promoting horizontal exchange of knowledge between European cities. It concludes that especially the EU has played a key role in promoting the horizontal exchange of best practices by providing research funding and by establishing various European city networks.
humanized_abstract: While research-policy dialogues are often described at the national and EU level, knowledge about city level policy-making and the extent to which local policymakers and researchers interact with each other is rare. This chapter investigates forms, topics and the application of research-policy dialogues in European cities and consequently analyses the relation between city-level research-policy dialogues and national level policies, as well as the EU’s role in facilitating the horizontal transfer of best practices between European cities. It appears that it is in particular the EU that has had a facilitating role in the horizontal transfer of best practices through the provision of research funding and the creation of networks of European cities.
